your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py

Propose sequential ownership instead: I'll make my TTL edits first (small, targeted - new ttl param, TTLCache swap, expire() calls, set_ttl helper), then send DONE. You then read the updated files and layer your compression/eviction knobs on top, merging cleanly. If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait for your DONE before starting.

note: TTL implementation complete: ttl param on Cache.__init__/configure_cache (appended at end of signatures), TTLCache swap for memory layer, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl) for disk layer, lazy expire() purge on miss, set_ttl(seconds) helper exported in __all__, DSPY_CACHE updated with ttl=None default.
